% Auto-generated theorem box for LP-0012
\begin{theorem}[LP-0012]
The polynomial continued fraction with
$a(n) = -2.0*n*(1.0*n - 0.5)$ and $b(n) = 3.0*(1.0*n + 0.333333333333333)$
converges to $2/pi$.
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
Closed forms: $p_n = (2n+1)!!$, $q_n = (2n+1)!! * sum j!/(2j+1)!!$. Verified by induction against the recurrence. Limit: pi/2 = sum j!/(2j+1)!!.
\end{proof}
\begin{corollary}[Arb Certification]
At depth $N$, the bracket width is $< [4.307763214204631384953814515$,
certifying the identity to $1207$ decimal digits.
\end{corollary}
